claims.claim: The risk of explosion increases with increasing state of charge (SOC) for 18650 lithium-ion batteries in hermetic space.
claims.quote: the experimental results showed that the risk of explosion upgrading with the increase of SOC

claims.claim: Before the explosion, H2 concentration reached a peak of 1628 ppm (0.1628%) and CO reached 668 ppm (0.0668%), both far below their lower flammability limits of 4.0% and 12.5% respectively, indicating H2 and CO are not the main trigger of the explosion.
claims.quote: the concentration of H2 and CO increased sharply and reached peaks of 1628ppm (0.1628%) and 668ppm (0.0668%) respectively before the explosion... The results showed that the contents of H2 and CO before the explosion are far below their LFLs (4.0% and 12.5%), indicating that H2 and CO are not the main trigger of the explosion.

claims.claim: Internal short circuit events increased the vaporization rate of electrolytes, with the amplitude and rate of voltage drop caused by internal short circuit gradually increasing with continuous overcharge.
claims.quote: the amplitude and rate of voltage drop caused by the internal short circuit gradually increased. In other words, the ability of battery to withstand overcharge gradually reduced. The degree of internal short circuit gradually increased, which manifested the battery module was approaching the thermal runaway state constantly.

claims.claim: When the overpressure in the central container reached a peak of 20 kPa, the pressure on the pressure relief plates of the adjacent container at 3 m on the opposite side reached a peak of 4.5 kPa, which may push open those plates and allow direct high-temperature or flame influence on the interior of the adjacent container.
claims.quote: When the overpressure in the container reached the peak value of 20kPa, the pressure on the 8# relief pressure plates reached the peak value of 4.5kPa. That is to say, when the setting of pressure relief plate was consistent, the PRPs at 3m on the opposite side may be pushed away by the explosion overpressure, resulting in the direct influence of high-temperature or even flame on the interior of the adjacent container.

claims.claim: In the numerical study of the ESS explosion, the area above 343 K was larger at a low altitude layer of 0.4 m than at heights of 1.5 m or 2.8 m, and the longest expansion distance of the high-temperature zone was about 8 m in the passage perpendicular to the container door.
claims.quote: The area above 343K in Fig. 10c was larger than that in Fig. 10a and 10b... The longest expansion distance of the high-temperature zone was about 8m in the passage perpendicular to the container door.

claims.claim: At 0% SOC, the maximum battery surface temperature during thermal runaway heating was only 277.8°C, and thermal runaway did not occur.
claims.quote: the maximum temperature of 0%SOC is only 277.8 ◦C, and thermal runaway will not even occur.

claims.claim: With increasing SOC, the onset time of thermal runaway advances, the onset temperature decreases significantly, the maximum temperature increases significantly, and thermal safety decreases significantly.
claims.quote: with the further increase of charge, the occurrence time of thermal runaway of the battery is gradually advanced, the occurrence temperature of thermal runaway decreases significantly, the maximum temperature increases significantly, and the thermal safety decreases significantly.

claims.claim: The maximum temperatures reached during thermal runaway were 277.8°C, 506.6°C, 550.8°C, 645.5°C, and 777.7°C for 0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, and 100% SOC batteries, respectively.

With increasing SOC, the lower explosion limit (LEL) of thermal runaway exhaust gas shows the same trend as alkane content, increasing first and then decreasing, while the upper explosion limit (UEL) shows the same trend as unsaturated hydrocarbon content, decreasing first and then increasing.
claims.quote: with the increase of SOC, the change of lower explosion limit (LEL) shows the same trend as that in alkanes content, which increases and then decreases; while the change of upper explosion limit (UEL) shows the same trend as that in unsaturated hydrocarbons content, which decreases and then increases.

claims.claim: The 50% SOC battery had the highest LEL at 10.98% and the lowest UEL at 40.06%, indicating the lowest exhausted gas combustion explosion danger among the tested SOC groups.
claims.quote: the lower explosion limit of 50% SOC battery pyrolysis gas is the highest, at 10.98%, and the upper explosion limit is the lowest, at 40.06%.

claims.claim: For a charged (4.2 V) 18650 Sony LiCoO2 Li-ion battery tested in a VSP2 adiabatic calorimeter without a buffer tank, the maximum temperature (Tmax) and maximum pressure (Pmax) during thermal runaway reached 903.0 °C and 1565.9 psig, respectively, resulting in a thermal explosion with a heat of reaction of 26.2 kJ.
claims.quote: The Tmax and Pmax of the charged Li-ion battery during the runaway reaction reach 903.0 ◦C and 1565.9 psig (pound-force per square inch gauge), respectively. This result leads to a thermal explosion, and the heat of reaction is 26.2 kJ.

claims.claim: The heat of reaction for charged (4.2 V) 18650 LiCoO2 Li-ion batteries as determined by VSP2 adiabatic experiments was 18.55 ± 0.55 kJ, which is much higher than for uncharged (3.7 V) batteries.
claims.quote: The heat of reaction for the charged Li-ion batteries as determined by VSP2 experiments was 18.55 ± 0.55 kJ, which is much higher than for the uncharged batteries.

claims.claim: The porous separator breaks down at temperatures above 120 °C and then induces an internal short circuit in Li-ion batteries.
claims.quote: the porous separator breaks down at temperatures above 120 ◦C and then induces an internal short circuit

claims.claim: The thermal reactive hazards of charged (4.2 V) Li-ion batteries exponentially increased at approximately 200 °C, while uncharged (3.7 V) batteries did not exhibit such a sharp trend.
claims.quote: The thermal reactive hazards of the charged Li-ion batteries exponentially increased at approximately 200 ◦C, while the uncharged batteries did not have such a sharp trend.

claims.claim: The lowest initial exothermic temperature (T0) among charged 18650 LiCoO2 batteries tested was that of the Samsung battery at 116.5 °C, indicating the earliest onset of self-heating.
claims.quote: The lowest T0 was that of the Samsung charged Li-ion battery at 116.5 ◦C. The Samsung also had the earliest initial self-heating.
